focus_strategies.description: Use the Pomodoro technique (25 minutes work, 5 minutes break) or the 10/3 rule to maintain focus.
Clear your workspace, use headphones to block noise, and keep your phone in another room.
Break large projects into small, manageable steps to avoid feeling overwhelmed.
Exercise or take short walks to boost neurotransmitters and reduce restlessness.
Utilize fidget toys or listen to instrumental music/ambient noise to keep your brain engaged.
Use whiteboards, post-it notes, and digital apps (like Todoist) to track tasks and appointments.
Use a 'thought dump' notebook to jot down unrelated thoughts and revisit them later.

habits_to_support_focus.description: Establish a predictable daily schedule to reduce cognitive stress.
Use the 'two-minute rule'—if a task takes less than two minutes, do it immediately.
Maintain a balanced diet, as this can affect energy levels and symptoms.
